title: FSM state (pipeline current-state file)
description: fsm-state.json at run root. Durable current state of the pipeline FSM (see references/state-machine.md). Models the phase, the executor<->verifier loop substate (Q = EXECUTE|VERIFY|ADJUDICATE|RETRY|ESCALATE|DONE), the bounded retries counter (max 2), gate flags, and per-unit status. The single top-level `loop` object tracks the MOST RECENTLY TRANSITIONED unit; because units run in parallel waves, each `units[]` item MAY additionally carry its own durable per-unit `retries` + `loop_state` (D-02/IMP-11). Loop cross-check iteration<=retries+1 is enforced in validate_run.py for both the `loop` slot and every `units[]` item that records `retries`.

model.description: Optional: the model this run executed under (recorded so model-scoped learnings can bind). Absent => unrecorded.

validator_version.description: OPTIONAL (F1/WP-E). The plugin/validator version that scaffolded this run (init_run.sh stamps it from .claude-plugin/plugin.json). Enables the version-skew policy (state-machine.md §5): the CURRENT validator's findings on a run stamped with an OLDER version are expected-skew, not defects. NEVER gates a check — an absent stamp (legacy/pre-1.7.0 runs) is judged exactly as before.

anchors_baseline_hash.description: OPTIONAL (1.10.0; schema-deltas §D). Ledger-side mirror of dialogues.json.anchors_baseline.content_hash. As of 1.10.1 it is CROSS-CHECKED by I39-7 when present: a present mirror MUST equal that content_hash (Limitation X hardening — a coordinated rewrite must then also rewrite fsm-state, raising the cost to three files; adoption-gated on presence, absent => silent). Absent => valid (the zero-delta primary posture; round counters + the anchor baseline live in dialogues.json, DP-21). DECLARED so the top-level additionalProperties:false is not violated (the lens2 rule); NEVER required (archive-safe) and NEVER gates a transition — still PRESERVES (GV-28).

signoff_confirmed.description: The human accepted the deliverable at the Phase-8 sign-off gate (G-signoff / T12). REQUIRED true to reach phase DONE — validate_run.py's REQUIRED_GATES lists it for DONE, so a run at DONE without it is INVALID (D-06). Like personas_confirmed, the flag is a human attestation the validator checks the PRESENCE of, not the genuineness of.

loop.description: Executor<->verifier loop substate for the MOST RECENTLY TRANSITIONED unit in Phase 6. With parallel waves >1 unit may be in-flight; this single slot is a back-compat snapshot of the last unit to transition, while each unit's durable per-unit loop state lives in its `units[]` item (`retries` + `loop_state`) — D-02/IMP-11. retries is HARD-BOUNDED at 2 (termination invariant; variant V=2-retries). state uses the loop's Q vocabulary.

items.description: Per-unit durable record. `retries` + `loop_state` are OPTIONAL (D-02/IMP-11): they make each in-flight unit's correction-loop substate representable during parallel waves, instead of only the single top-level `loop` slot. When `retries` is present, validate_run.py cross-checks the unit's verify.iteration <= retries+1 (I4, same bound as the loop slot).

expansion.description: OPTIONAL (Bounded Graph Amendments). The Phase-6 graph-amendment fuel budget. Seeded at Phase 4 (default min(N0,8); a non-default value comes only from an explicit instruction at an earlier gate — decomposition has no human touchpoint, U2/D-C: exactly three human gates). 0 or absent => BGA disabled (today's behavior). fuel is monotone-decreasing: only an amendment writes it, mirroring the retries counter. validate_run.py I18 cross-checks fuel_remaining == fuel_initial - sum(amendment fuel_cost) and fuel_remaining >= 0. Top-level is additionalProperties:false, so this property must be declared.

depth.description: OPTIONAL (depth tiers). Human-gated run depth tier, confirmed at the Phase-1 persona gate (rides the existing gate — no new gate flag; REQUIRED_GATES untouched). tier = EFFECTIVE tier; confirmed_tier/proposed_tier are the immutable gate record; skipped_floors is the canonical DT-K<n>@<confirmed_tier> delta disclosure ([] only for full), re-rendered for the CONFIRMED tier on divergent confirmation; phase2_touch records the unconditional Phase-2 keep-or-raise touch; overrides[] is append-only and UPWARD-only (light<standard<full) with a DECISIONS.md ref + pending_units snapshot per entry — the orchestrator can never lower a confirmed tier, and raised floors bind only units not yet PASS-verified (per-unit tier_at_verification in verify). Absent => mechanism not adopted (validator I28 silent — archive-safe). Top-level is additionalProperties:false, so this property must be declared.
